As an athlete, nothing compares to the grueling training sessions that lead up to match day. When I stepped away, I found that, for me—having put my entire body on steroids for an hour, four sessions, and three days—the most gratifying physical achievement would only be the ones I completed after my last session in Mexico.

I don't necessarily blame coaches who allow their players to take drugs. These players—uneducated, ignorant, or lazy—were either on drugs, or they needed more money to stay in school. These coaches weren't in a position of moral opprobrium over these violations. To their eternal credit, however, many of these coaches tried to explain those practices to their athletes and got help along the way as needed, which was always necessary under any circumstance. These coaches were also very proactive and helpful. Yet, in spite of their support, no sport has yet truly eliminated a player's need for performance-enhancing substances. While we must strive, the temptation will always remain as strong as it ever was—it only takes one young player with an appetite.

Crossing the finish line after a grueling marathon is a feeling I will never forget. My legs were burning, but the excitement to finally see another runner and feel the glow of crossing the finish line did it for me. With another two months in the mountains, a long trail, more food, extra water and so much love from my extended network (I was the only woman on Sunday, which helped a ton, and I still have many friendships on the other trails), the final stretch started out as a real long day, until I found myself on the trail running with several new faces I can't properly thank you all enough.

As soon as they started jogging, I asked why weren't they a marathoner instead. They both rolled their eyes, but quickly realized I couldn't hold my answers in, so the discussion quickly shifted from just why it's cool being part of one of the few women's (or only ultra marathon) communities. After having spent just two months at the trail, these strangers from all over the USA had already taken an enormous leap to make that community a more welcoming place. Caffeine can suppress melatonin secretion causing disrupted homeostatic wake-sleep cycles while having little or no apparent effect in free wake. The end product of my research using analog flashcards on an elearning learning management system as a teacher and instructor for two decades proved that when learners could access the text's text content during their learning sessions using the elearning teaching app (aka 'digital textbook-to-realware application'), students performed significantly less poor comprehension, word recall retention and increased test scores overall.

Why Learning Management Technology?

As students increasingly take up elearning as their primary mode of online learning and as the role of the college/ university increases over a decade as an educational and research outlet for university faculty, then a key role is increasingly becoming learning management. My experience in working with students and university faculty involved in this emerging practice of elearning has given me, a seasoned learner educator and educational software maker, the insight that these issues deserve to be dealt with much differently

A structured approach to academic learning focuses on conceptual understanding before attempting rote memorization of skills. Instead, the learning model encourages new learning behavior—that is, learning that allows participants to experience something new by experimenting.

An integrated approach, by contrast, provides access to rote instruction as needed throughout a semester based solely on what the student knows. It typically involves less direct coaching, and the individual instruction in terms of curriculum are flexible, tailored to a student's particular learning ability. Such instruction, in essence, seeks more flexibility between learning activity in a session, rather than total freedom between learning task; as a result the student feels more supported and can better apply a concept's implications.

Both options require a shift in orientation away from self-directed knowledge acquisition (SWBA), as embodied by many academic institutions throughout academic-industrial sectors in the Western democracies, and a more integrated understanding to be able to successfully participate in both formal-informal learning in university contexts and everyday learning within the world.

Crowdcasting is yet another example of how crowdfunding is leading to a new reality: If not enough money is raised — whether with individual investors or through crowd funding — there still tends to exist some level of independent-mindedness, resulting in quality that is often lower than the quality in the conventional market. And so those who choose not to put themselves solely/completely behind some production may find that they still will have control over the production without actually having their funding being taken away — and sometimes they might just produce the highest-quality output they possible can when the chance at financial security is on the line, as in case of Indie Game-Maker itself.

The idea behind crowdfunding isn't to have your money gone up in flames all due to an unknown situation that your company might face, either; you'll never feel a loss just when a crowdfunding campaign reaches the total amount they need. Debates over international trade tariffs remain a core topic of discussion among leading political economics professors, such as Paul Krugman and Brad DeLong, but these issues appear peripheral to the ongoing public debate between economic left and right. I therefore argue the issue of tariffs is of high practical relevance, but we must understand this for our analysis with great caution. A first argument used on this matter of interest is as simple as "people don't buy more stuff if their competitors import less". One important consequence of being too far advanced toward free trade in manufacturing can also include overpricing which in turn could limit market share. For example, US producers often claim an average product prices 40 to 50 percent higher than a German steelmaker.
